item.title: Re: Provisional Licensing Information
item.content:encoded: As the first image could not be sourced and the second image comes from here, the images have been removed for being non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0).

item.title: Provisional Licensing Information
item.content:encoded:
Note: Image source of "tumblr_mdy8gqHYd01ry5s4ko1_500.jpg" and "eye_silkscreen.png" are still unknown.

  • cctv.jpg

Source: https://www.flickr.com/photos/96526303@N00/4298498531/
License: CC BY 2.0

Title: He's watching you
Author: Sarah
Uploaded on: 2010

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is going in my best SCP folder

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All they should need to contain these guys is some Pepsi.

Joking aside, good entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed this. Well written, punchy and subtly creepy. Definitely +1 from me.

I do wonder though, what would happen if an area was no longer a part of the UK, like if Northern Ireland joined the Republic or Scotland gains independence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea of the SCP Foundation basically going "Oh, you can only show up where we can't see? We'll look at everything, then."
Well deserved for a great article. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted for "Instances of SCP-2884-1 appear only in locations outside of the view of pre-existing video surveillance equipment".

It would've been extremely easy to write an article saying "anomalous security cameras are popping up across the UK; the Foundation is encouraging the development of a surveillance state to disguise the anomalous cameras with real ones, because it can't stop more from appearing." The decision to instead write "anomalous security cameras are popping up across the UK; the Foundation is encouraging the development of a surveillance state to intercept the anomalous cameras with real ones, and thereby stop more from appearing" produces something significantly more interesting.

Weirdly enough, it's also more optimistic. We haven't been able to observe where these things come from or how they get here… and we've turned that fact into a weapon against them. That's awesome; go team.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is SO good to see that it's possible to make a GOOD skip out of a coldpost.
Wait, this is coming out all wrong…
Mmmh…
Take 2

People out there (including me), it is possible to coldpost a good skip, but you need a very much big huge amount of talent to manage it. And everyone is not Zacharia H.

+1 and special congratulations!

EDIT WOW. Everyone who voted upvoted!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it's because Britain has a reputation for being a bit of a "surveillance society" - supposedly, there's one security camera for every eleven people there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Overall a good job with this. However, I only wish that the setting be in China instead, since their state television is also called CCTV.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this SCP! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: brilliant work tying those two together, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did anyone else get the ad for CCTV Surveillance systems at the bottom of the page, and got just a little creeped out?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oddly enough, I felt the most British line was "Never had them. Don't need them."

(It's brilliant, by the way. +1.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wouldn't know because I'm not from the UK, but the civilian captured in the "arrest" incident seems British in a somewhat exaggerated, comical way. (Specifically, "Oi", "bloody", and "fucking tossers") If I'm wrong about that then just ignore this comment.

Other than that, this is pleasantly off-putting (Can that be a thing?) and a good, simple SCP. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man, this is a good example of both "real-world badness explained by anomalies" and "not quite human things". And I'm pleased by this, because "security cameras appearing everywhere" could have been a skip all by itself, but combining it with the freaky riot police makes this so much better.

I will say that "The Overseer is watching you right now." isn't the greatest zinger (I mean, I kinda figured that was true, he's got a camera on his head right there), but that's more or less made up for by the -2's insistence that he doesn't need eyes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted when I realized that the the first picture wasn't a pathetic attempt at horror, but a flock of security cameras. A whole flock of them. Also, wasn't the original 1984 book set in London?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 1002 was the one I was thinking of

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it how this is set in the UK (in London for example, where cameras capture each citizien like 300 times a day). +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I upvoted because I love the tone and quality of this, as well as it being a fairly unique idea.

That said, the incident log near the end made me laugh a bit. Darkly humorous, if only a little dark.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not retracting my upvote or anything but I will say "the Overseer" concept and "who's watching you?" are pretty corny and cliched. Good work for being able to salvage an entertaining SCP out of such a stock idea, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hm? It's a reference to the same song WrongJohnSilver was quoting in his post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love this kind of stuff. Upvoted.

(Spontaneously arrested for upvoting excellent skip without a permit)

item.title: Staffpost- Closed
item.content:encoded: Doctor Coyle, this comment is pointless, needlessly confrontational, and has nothing to do with the article at hand. I'm closing off this conversation here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Only thing that bothers me is this:

individual/group/entity known as "The Overseer"

I would prefer something like POI-1984 "The Overseer"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Christianity is stupid. Communism is good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also note the number. And the 84 personnel. And the M84.

84.
84.
There are no subliminal messages here.

Shop as usual. And avoid panic buying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm the opposite; I would have preferred less info on the Overseer and wasn't too fond of the "he's watching you" stinger. All in all, though, I thought this article is great. It uses the idea of mass surveillance to instill a great sense of paranoia despite the relatively slim danger of the anomaly (as long as you keep your shoes on near cameras, anyhow).

Telling how an SCP is going to murder you gruesomely isn't very creepy. This article doesn't tell you what this will do to you, when it will do it and most of all why it will do it. The fact that this is told using a perverted idea of the law (where the law is seemingly arbitrary but no less inflexible) makes it even more effective.

Also, don't tell me the -Bs don't have a little bit of Judge Dredd in them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-973 and SCP-1002, but I think this is distinct enough to coexist with them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I may be wrong but I think there is already an SCP similar to this (anomalous policemen that disproportionately punish people for nonsensical crimes)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wonderfully creepy. I may be wrong, but are

Mobile Task Force Theta-11 ("Big Brother")

and

"Who's watching you?"

1984 references?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty darn great. While I'm not sure the "overseer" explanation was entirely satisfying as a reveal, the anomaly itself and the way the -2's acted is more than enough for me to enjoy the piece and justify my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Noice. held my attention.

SCP-2884-2-A: Never had them. Don't need them.

Dr. Carlisle: Are you sure? Because from here, without your visor, they look like they've been surgically removed.

SCP-2884-2-A: Never had them. [subject leans forward slightly] Don't need them.

lmao this shit was great

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great. I totally love the concept, and it is cool you found so many images for the article.

A very solid +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Impressive, especially for a coldpost after five hours of writing..

item.title: Re: Image Sources
item.content:encoded:
Warning received. Here's the link to the header on the VRwiki, if it helps:

http://visual-records.wdfiles.com/local--files/machine/tumblr_mdy8gqHYd01ry5s4ko1_500.jpg

item.title: Re: Image Sources
item.content:encoded: Be wary of the Visual Records wiki. It is a dark and unattributed place.

item.title: Image Sources
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2884 is up. The header image is from the Foundation Visual Records archive. Sources for the other two images:

http://lucidtronix.com/system/attached_files/119/original/eye_silkscreen.png?1359518190
http://theunboundedspirit.com/wp-content/uploads/2012/03/cctv.jpg
